hyperedges.label: Java LSP Intelligence Stack (browser to JDT LS)
AdvantageScope Lite NT4 Hosting and Display

nodes.label: VS Code Dark Modern Token Colors
nodes.norm_label: vs code dark modern token colors

nodes.label: Decision 004: Backend Wiring for Save and Run
nodes.norm_label: decision 004: backend wiring for save and run

nodes.label: Rationale: Headless sim via omission (no SimGUI/DriverStation)
nodes.rationale: GradleRIO 2026 makes SimGUI and DriverStation sim opt-in; removing those lines from build.gradle avoids needing an X server or DISPLAY env var.
nodes.norm_label: rationale: headless sim via omission (no simgui/driverstation)

nodes.label: Rationale: AS Lite reads window.location.hostname for NT4
nodes.rationale: When ASCOPE_DISTRIBUTION=LITE, AS Lite hardcodes window.location.hostname:5810 for NT4, so serving on localhost:8080 with sim on localhost:5810 works with zero source patches.
nodes.norm_label: rationale: as lite reads window.location.hostname for nt4

nodes.label: Rationale: Custom WebSocket sender without @fastify/websocket
nodes.rationale: The /run socket is server-to-client text streaming only; the backend implements WebSocket handshake and unmasked text frames directly on Node HTTP upgrade event, avoiding a dependency.

nodes.label: Rationale: Plain Monaco client with direct LSP requests instead of TypeFox wrappers
nodes.rationale: TypeFox MonacoVscodeApiWrapper caused unreliable browser behavior; a small direct LSP adapter in java-lsp.ts is simpler and more debuggable for MVP scope.
nodes.norm_label: rationale: plain monaco client with direct lsp requests instead of typefox wrappers

nodes.label: Rationale: No Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy headers (AS Lite iframe)
nodes.rationale: Adding COEP caused Chrome to reject the AS Lite iframe from localhost:8080, so cross-origin isolation headers were deliberately left off the Vite dev server.
nodes.norm_label: rationale: no cross-origin-embedder-policy headers (as lite iframe)

nodes.label: Rationale: AS Lite embedded via iframe, not bundled into web app
nodes.rationale: iframe sandboxes AS Lite's WebGL/wasm/asset loading, avoids coupling build pipelines, and auto-connects to NT4 via window.location.hostname.
nodes.norm_label: rationale: as lite embedded via iframe, not bundled into web app

nodes.label: Rationale: tini as PID 1 in sim container to reap zombie processes
nodes.rationale: Without an init reaper, stopped sim PIDs remained as zombies and caused every rebuild to wait for the 10-second hard-kill path.
nodes.norm_label: rationale: tini as pid 1 in sim container to reap zombie processes

nodes.label: Rationale: Single-stage Dockerfile to preserve Gradle cache
nodes.rationale: Multi-stage builds would lose ~/.gradle/caches; baking the cache in a single stage is necessary so container startup does not re-download WPILib jars.
nodes.norm_label: rationale: single-stage dockerfile to preserve gradle cache

nodes.label: NT4 Routing under Multi-Tenancy (spike question)
nodes.rationale: AS Lite hardcodes window.location.hostname:5810; multi-tenant options include port-per-user, subdomain-per-user, WS proxy demux by path, or patching AS Lite to read endpoint from URL param.

nodes.label: Shared vs. Per-user JDT LS topology (spike question)
nodes.rationale: Eclipse JDT LS supports multiple workspaces in one process. Shared instance saves ~1.5 GB RAM per student if it works with correct per-browser routing and failure isolation.
nodes.norm_label: shared vs. per-user jdt ls topology (spike question)
